name: WalkMe Digital Adoption Platform API
what_it_does: WalkMe enterprise digital adoption platform REST API for enterprise organizations to create and manage in-app guidance, employee training workflows, process automation, and usage analytics across web applications with AI-powered content recommendations. Enables AI agents to manage walk-through and smart tip creation for guided onboarding automation, handle content deployment and publication for digital guidance automation, access usage analytics and adoption metrics for digital transformation measurement automation, retrieve user behavior and flow analytics for adoption gap automation, manage segment and targeting rule configuration for personalized guidance automation, handle A/B test and variant management for guidance optimization automation, access AI-powered content suggestion for onboarding improvement automation, retrieve integration with SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and enterprise apps for DAP deployment automation, manage survey and feedback collection for adoption assessment automation, and integrate WalkMe with enterprise HRIS, LMS, and analytics platforms for end-to-end digital adoption workflow.
use_cases: Creating employee training guides from digital adoption automation agents
Measuring software adoption from analytics automation agents
Deploying in-app guidance from personalized onboarding agents
Automating enterprise app workflows from process automation agents
not_for: Product-led growth consumer onboarding (use Appcues or Intercom for PLG)
Simple product tours for SaaS (use Chameleon or Userflow for lightweight tours)
Learning management systems (use Cornerstone or Docebo for LMS)
best_when: An enterprise organization wanting AI agents to automate employee digital training, adoption analytics, personalized in-app guidance, and process automation across SAP, Salesforce, and enterprise web applications through WalkMe's enterprise DAP platform.
avoid_when: ENTERPRISE IMPLEMENTATION REQUIRES PROFESSIONAL SERVICES: WalkMe enterprise deployment requires professional services for content strategy, governance, and technical implementation; automated self-service WalkMe integration requires enterprise contract and PS engagement; automated standalone implementation without WalkMe content strategy creates low-adoption guidance deployments. API ACCESS LIMITED VS WALKME CONSOLE CAPABILITIES: WalkMe REST API provides access to analytics, content management, and deployment; some advanced DAP features (live walkthroughs, automated actions) have limited API control vs WalkMe Console; automated content creation may need to supplement with WalkMe Editor for advanced guidance types. WALKME SNIPPET INSTALLATION REQUIRED ON TARGET APPS: WalkMe deployment requires JavaScript snippet installation on target web applications; automated WalkMe guidance requires target app to include WalkMe snippet; automated guidance on apps without WalkMe snippet cannot display in-app guidance regardless of API configuration.

auth.notes: WalkMe uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for integrations. REST API with JSON. San Francisco, California HQ (Tel Aviv Israel R&D). Founded 2011 by Raphael Sweary and Eyal Cohen. Acquired by SAP in 2023 for $1.5B. Products: Digital adoption platform, employee training, process automation, analytics, AI recommendations. Integrations: Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow, Workday, Oracle. GDPR. SOC2. Serves 2,000+ global enterprises. Competes with Whatfix, Appcues, and Pendo for digital adoption.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise sales with professional services
pricing.paid_tiers: Custom enterprise pricing; typically $50K+/year for mid-enterprise; larger deployments $200K+/year
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. SAP subsidiary. Custom enterprise pricing. $50K+/year. Annual contract with PS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; analytics and content queries throttled; webhooks for completion, engagement, and survey events

known_agent_gotchas: SYSTEM ID REQUIRED FOR ALL CONTENT REQUESTS: WalkMe API is scoped to System (a WalkMe representation of a target web application); all content and analytics requests require System ID; automated multi-app DAP management must maintain System ID registry per target application

CONTENT PUBLICATION vs DRAFT STATE FOR DEPLOYMENT: WalkMe content (walk-throughs, smart tips) has draft and published states; automated guidance deployment must explicitly publish content after creation; automated content creation without publication step creates draft content invisible to end users

ANALYTICS DATA FRESHNESS LAG: WalkMe analytics data has processing lag (typically 24 hours) before appearing in API; automated adoption reporting must account for data freshness; automated same-day usage reporting may return incomplete metrics for recent user sessions

SAP ACQUISITION ROADMAP UNCERTAINTY: WalkMe's product roadmap and API strategy under SAP ownership is evolving; automated integrations should monitor for API version changes as WalkMe integrates into SAP ecosystem; automated standalone WalkMe deployments may shift toward SAP-integrated deployment model

WALKME EDITOR vs API FOR CONTENT CREATION PARITY: WalkMe Editor (browser extension for content creation) supports richer guidance types than API; automated content creation via API cannot create all guidance types available in Editor; automated content generation should verify API support for target guidance type before implementation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise DAP. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Employee training, adoption, and behavioral data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise DAP. GDPR, SOC2. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Employee and behavioral data.
